question.text:
An articulation point in connected graph is a vertex 
such that removing the vertex and its incident edges 
disconnects the graph into two or more connected 
components.  

Let T be a DFS tree obtained by doing DFS in 
connected undirected graph G.  

Which of the following options is/are correct?

options.text:
Root of T can never be an articulation point in 
G.

Root of T is an articulation point in G if and only 
if it has 2 or more children.
A leaf of T can be an articulation point in G.

If u is an articulation point in G such that x is an 
ancestor of u in T and y is a descendent of u in 
T, then all paths from x to y in G must pass 
through u.

solution.text:
If the Root has only one child it can never 
disconnect a tree on its removal. Therefore, option 
(b) is correct.

headers: If the Root has only one child it can never
rows: disconnect a tree on its removal. Therefore, option

question.text:
Consider a complete binary tree with 7 nodes. Let A 
denote the set of first 3 elements obtained by 
performing Breadth-first search (BFS) starting from 
the root. Let B denote the set of first 3 elements 
obtained by performing Depth - First Search (DFS) 
starting from the root.  

The value of |A - B | is _________.

Let G be a simple undirected graph. Let TD be a depth 
first search tree of G. Let TB be a breadth first search 
tree of G. Consider the following statements: 

I: No edge of G is a cross edge with respect to TD. 
(A cross edge in G is between two nodes neither 
of which is an ancestor of the other in TD). 

II: For every edge (u, ╧Ζ) of G, if u is at depth i and 
╧Ζ is at depth j in TB, then |i -j| = 1.  

Which of the statements above must necessarily be 
true?

solution.text:
έΑλ 
No edge of G is a cross edge with respect to TD. 
(A cross edge in G is between two nodes neither 
of which is an ancestor of the other in TD). 
TRUE 
έΑλ 
For every edge (u, ╧Ζ) of G, if u is at depth i and 
╧Ζ is at depth j in TB, then |i -j| = 1. FALSE

question.text:
Let G = (V, E) be a simple undirected graph, and s 
be particular vertex in it called the source. For  
x ΎΔΟ V, let d(x) denote the shortest distance in G from 
s to x. A breadth first search (BFS) is performed 
starting at s. Let T be the resultant BFS tree. If (u, v) 
is an edge of G that is not in T, then which one of the 
following CANNOT be the value of d(u) - d(v)?

solution.text: 2 cannot be the value of d(u) - d(v).

question.text:
Suppose depth first search is executed on the graph 
below starting at some unknown vertex. Assume that 
a recursive call to visit a vertex is made only after 
first checking that the vertex has not been visited 
earlier. Then the maximum possible recursion depth 
(including the initial call) is ______.

solution.text:
There exists total 21 nodes and only 2 nodes require 
back track therefore the maximum recursion depth 
is 21 - 2 = 19.

question.text:
Consdier the directed graph given below: 


Which of one the following is TRUE?

options.text:
The graph does not have any topological 
ordering.

Both PQRS and SRQP are topological 
orderings.

orderings.

solution.text:
The topological orderings PSRQ and SPRQ are 
identical.
